steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's stated goal is to provide an example of one of Catlett's sculptures specifically—not prints, not general descriptions, not biographical facts. The correct choice must name a specific work and identify it as a sculpture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The print *Dancing* was created by celebrated artist Elizabeth Catlett in 1990.’: Dancing is explicitly listed in the notes as a print, not a sculpture. This directly fails the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Artist Elizabeth Catlett is best known for creating sculptures and prints that explore the Black experience.’: This is a general description of Catlett's work and names no specific sculpture. It does not provide an example of a sculpture. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The sculpture *Star Gazer* was created by celebrated artist Elizabeth Catlett in 1997.’: Correct. The notes identify Star Gazer as a 1997 sculpture by Catlett. This choice names the work, correctly labels it a sculpture, and gives the year. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Elizabeth Catlett, a celebrated artist, was born in 1915.’: This is biographical information only. It names no artwork at all, let alone a sculpture. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
